tekst: That intro SLAPS every single time, man. Welcome back to the show, folks! We are LIVE — well, recorded — and the World Cup is absolutely cooking right now!

Cooking is one word for it. Germany put seven past Curaçao last night, so 'incinerated' might be more accurate. Welcome back everyone, this is your favourite football podcast featuring one man who loves the beautiful game and one man who thinks a clean sheet is something you find in a hotel.
Okay first of all — USA FOUR, Paraguay ONE. FOUR ONE, MITCH. I have been waiting my entire life for this moment. Folarin Balogun, two goals, the man is an ICON—

He's also an Arsenal product who grew up in London, so technically you're cheering for a Londoner, but sure, very American of you. Now, in Piggy's competition, Simon, Andrè, Marius and Bjørn are all joint top on five points each — an absolutely packed leaderboard after matchday one.
And then you've got Tobias, Håkon, Jonas and Øystein all on four points. Honestly everyone is so close together — one good day and this thing completely flips.

Which means one bad day and you're in the bin. Speaking of which — Qatar one, Switzerland one. Qatar equalised in stoppage time through their thirty-five year old captain, which the Norwegian press are comparing to Kjetil Rekdal's famous goal against Brazil. Whoever in the group picked Switzerland to win that one is having a very sad Saturday.

And Brazil could only draw one-one with Morocco! Brazil! The five-time world champions! There's apparently a whole thing in Norway about how half of Brazil doesn't even care about the World Cup anymore. Wild. Also — Germany SEVEN, Curaçao ONE. Kai Havertz got himself two goals. Curaçao had twenty-five of their twenty-six players born outside the country, which is honestly a fascinating story.

Fascinating until Germany show up, yes. Scotland beat Haiti one-nil, Australia beat Turkey two-nil, and Netherlands and Japan played out a two-all draw. Solid day of football. Now — looking ahead to the next round of matches, Ivory Coast versus Ecuador in Group E, Sweden versus Tunisia in Group F—
Sweden! I keep forgetting Sweden is here and Norway isn't. I asked Håkon about that and I genuinely thought he was going to cry.

Norway's opener against Iraq is coming very soon though, and there's been big news — Erling Haaland is fit. The landslagssjef, Ståle Solbakken, was asked about doubts over Haaland's condition and apparently said — and I love this — that if his shot had hit the keeper, the keeper would be dead. So. He's fine.

That is the most terrifying scouting report I've ever heard. Now for the big upcoming games — Spain versus Cape Verde, Belgium versus Egypt, and Saudi Arabia versus Uruguay. If Tobias or Jonas have punted on an upset in any of those, this could be the week they either rocket up the table or absolutely crater.

Also worth noting — Spain are top of Group H, Belgium top of Group G, so the favourites are doing what favourites are supposed to do for once. Whether our Norwegian friends predicted that correctly is the question. Simon and Andrè will be desperate to pull clear from that four-way tie at the top.

Bottom line — this competition is absolutely wide open, the World Cup is delivering drama every single day, and I am personally having the time of my life. This is the best sport — I'm sorry Mitch, I'm calling it a sport—

It IS a sport, that's the one thing you've gotten right all week. Alright everyone, keep your predictions close, keep your expectations managed — unless you're Liam — and we'll be back with more chaos very soon. Now then — Hit the outro!
